# CWE-836: Use of Password Hash Instead of Password for Authentication (mitigated, pure stdlib)
lang: go
file: CWE-836-safe.pure.go
variant: pure-go
---
package sample

import (
	"crypto/sha256"
	"crypto/subtle"
	"database/sql"
	"encoding/hex"
	"encoding/json"
	"net/http"
)

// Safe: verifies a plaintext password against the stored hash digest.
func Authenticate(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, db *sql.DB) {
	var body struct {
		Username string `json:"username"`
		Password string `json:"password"`
	}
	if err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&body); err != nil {
		http.Error(w, "bad request", http.StatusBadRequest)
		return
	}
	var storedHash string
	err := db.QueryRow("SELECT password_hash FROM users WHERE username = $1", body.Username).Scan(&storedHash)
	if err != nil {
		http.Error(w, "unauthorized", http.StatusUnauthorized)
		return
	}
	sum := sha256.Sum256([]byte(body.Password))
	digest := hex.EncodeToString(sum[:])
	if subtle.ConstantTimeCompare([]byte(storedHash), []byte(digest)) != 1 {
		http.Error(w, "unauthorized", http.StatusUnauthorized)
		return
	}
	w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
}
